---
name: New pitfall rule
about: Propose a new data pitfall for datapitfalls to detect
title: "[Pitfall]: "
labels: pitfall-rule
assignees: ""
---

> Thanks for helping grow the taxonomy! Before submitting, it helps to skim
> [docs/PITFALL_TAXONOMY.md](https://github.com/bjonesdataliteracy/datapitfalls/blob/main/docs/PITFALL_TAXONOMY.md)
> to see the rule format and a few worked examples.

## Pitfall Name

A short, memorable name for this pitfall (e.g. "Truncated Y-Axis", "Survivorship Bias").

## Audit Domain

_Which of the seven domains does this belong to? Choose one:_

- [ ] Epistemic Errors
- [ ] Technical Trespasses
- [ ] Mathematical Miscues
- [ ] Statistical Slip-Ups
- [ ] Analytical Aberrations
- [ ] Graphical Gaffes
- [ ] Design Dangers

## Description of the Pitfall

What is the pitfall? Why does it mislead or cause harm when working with data?

## How Should the Tool Detect It?

How might datapitfalls spot this — in a chart image, a code snippet, a plain-English description, or a document? Be as concrete as you can about the signals or patterns to look for.

## Example of This Pitfall in the Wild

A real or realistic example. A chart, a snippet of code, a sentence, a published claim — anything that shows the pitfall in action.

## Suggested Severity

- [ ] `info` — worth noting, not necessarily wrong
- [ ] `warning` — likely a problem, should be reviewed
- [ ] `error` — clearly misleading or incorrect

## References or Sources

Links, citations, book sections, or articles that document this pitfall.
